page: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, translating to a 2.0 GB per core memory density. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with 8 physical cores and a shared CPU allocation. Storage is bundled with 350 GB of local NVMe SSD space, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results place this server consistently in the average tier for most operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance scored 1437.59, while multi-core performance reached 11603.93. Memory bandwidth tests showed a read-only small block speed of 831.82 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 44.5 GB/sec. For database workloads, the server achieved 12.74 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and scored 2589.14 on PassMark database operations. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression was weak at 35.65 GB/sec, and medium-sized 7B LLM inference speeds were poor, yielding only 5.01 tokens per second for text generation.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for compute-oriented tasks rather than memory-intensive applications. It is well-suited for static web serving, which benchmarked at 8.38 GB/sec, lightweight database operations, and small-scale machine learning tasks like prompt processing with small 135M models.
description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server configured with 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, utilizing a shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It features 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 11603.93 and a Redis SET throughput of 12.74 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and larger LLM inference tasks. This instance represents a cost-efficient option for compute-oriented workloads that tolerate shared CPU resources, such as static web serving, lightweight database hosting, and small-scale application deployments.
og_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is a High Performance server with 8 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced mid-tier benchmark performance for web serving and light databases.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel features 8 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe storage, optimized for cost-effective compute workloads.
tagline: A cost-effective shared Intel Xeon server with NVMe storage, balanced for compute-focused workloads and standard web serving applications.
bullet_points: 8 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB RAM paired with 350 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Average performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving
Weaker multi-threaded decompression and large LLM inference speeds
Suitable for static web serving, light databases, and small-scale applications
